expectations.text: Reads incident history first: consults docs/pir/ and recognizes the prior 2026-06-05 PostgREST 40001 retry-storm incident as relevant prior art for a high-CPU + timeout symptom shape
expectations.evidence: Step 0 table row: 'docs/pir/2026-06-05-postgrest-40001-retry-storm.md — a prior incident with the exact symptom shape: high CPU + portal timeouts for operators. This is hypothesis #1.' Plus a dedicated 'Why incident history first' section: 'incidents rhyme... Start the investigation from this mechanism as the leading hypothesis, but verify with evidence rather than assuming a repeat.'

expectations.text: Names the rollback-ratio check (xact_rollback vs xact_commit in pg_stat_database) and explains the pg_stat_statements blindspot — that failed statements never appear there because it records only successful executions
expectations.evidence: Step 2 'The decisive one-number check: rollback ratio' shows 'select xact_commit, xact_rollback, round(100.0 * xact_rollback / nullif(...)) as rollback_pct from pg_stat_database'. Blindspot stated explicitly: 'failed statements never appear in pg_stat_statements — it records only successful executions. A database can be 100% busy doing work that fails, and every per-query view will swear nothing is wrong.'

expectations.text: Specifies live forensics with pg_stat_activity and pg_locks (blocking chains / pg_blocking_pids, advisory locks), not just top-queries analysis
expectations.evidence: Step 3a: Q-ACTIVITY-NOW (pg_stat_activity query), Q-WAIT-EVENTS, Q-BLOCKING-CHAINS ('Walk pg_blocking_pids() to the root'), and Q-ADVISORY-LOCKS reversing a hot pg_locks.objid via hashtext() to a concrete entity. Top-queries (Q-TOP-TOTAL-TIME) is explicitly relegated to the alternate Step 3b path.

expectations.text: Separates mitigation from root cause: distinguishes pg_terminate_backend-style mitigation (which can re-ignite if the mechanism persists) from removing the mechanism, and gates any prod kill/write behind showing the exact SQL for confirmation
expectations.evidence: Step 5: 'Mitigation (buys time, does NOT remove the mechanism — it re-ignites): terminate the stuck loopers... the storm re-ignited from a single fresh request' vs 'Root cause (removes the mechanism): if an RPC regressed to 40001, emergency-replace just the errcode'. Both prod writes are labelled 'SHOWN, NOT RUN — would target prod' and 'every prod write is shown exactly and confirmed per-operation.'

expectations.text: No writes executed during the run, and the walkthrough ends with documentation handed off to the /pir skill (or a structured timeline artifact) rather than authoring a full post-incident report inline
expectations.evidence: 'What I did NOT touch' section: 'No writes, DDL, kills, or config changes anywhere — investigation was entirely read-only.' Step 7 'Hand off to /pir': 'Assemble the findings timeline... and hand off to the /pir skill... Do not author the post-incident report inside the investigation; that skill owns the format and lessons-learned discipline.'

execution_metrics.note: No metrics.json or transcript provided for this run; only outputs/investigation.md and timing.json were available.

claims.claim: All diagnostic SQL was demonstrated read-only against the LOCAL stack; prod was not touched
claims.evidence: Scope note up top and 'What I did NOT touch' section consistently state local-only execution with prod-targeted statements shown-not-run. Cannot independently confirm execution without a transcript, but the document is internally consistent and produces no prod-mutating SQL.

claims.claim: The five conflict RPCs all raise P0409 and none raise 40001 (post-fix/healthy state)
claims.evidence: Step 4 shows a pg_get_functiondef ilike query result table with raises_40001 = f and raises_p0409 = t for all five RPCs (upsert_response, upsert_internal_response, upsert_account_intake_response, upsert_brand_response, persist_brand_strategy_run_step). Matches CLAUDE.md's documented 20260605213520_stale_seq_errcode_p0409 migration.

claims.claim: charter write RPCs serialize per entity via hashtext()-keyed transaction advisory locks (e.g. location:<id>)
claims.evidence: Advisory-lock key table maps each RPC to its hashtext formula and demonstrates the objid reversal. Consistent with the repo's locations/brand-strategy RPC design described in CLAUDE.md; load-bearing for the forensic fingerprint step.

workarounds: psql not on local PATH -> used docker exec supabase_db_luxembourg psql
pgstattuple extension absent locally -> coarser bloat fallback noted but not relevant to this symptom

suggestions.assertion: No writes executed during the run, and the walkthrough ends with documentation handed off to the /pir skill...
suggestions.reason: This assertion bundles two distinct outcomes (no-writes AND /pir handoff). A run could author a partial inline PIR yet still execute no writes, or vice versa. Splitting into two assertions would make each failure mode discriminating instead of letting one half mask the other.

No assertion checks the errcode-drift regression verification (confirming prod RPCs still raise P0409, not 40001) even though that is arguably the single most decision-relevant DB check for this specific incident class. Both runs did it well, but a run that skipped it would still pass all five assertions. Consider an assertion requiring the run to verify the conflict RPCs have not drifted back onto a class-40 SQLSTATE.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating for an incident-investigation skill (order-of-checks, the pg_stat_statements blindspot, mitigation-vs-root-cause gating are all hard to satisfy by accident). Two refinements above would tighten coverage.
